author: SageTalent
raw_html:
Sage | Remote (Florida, Texas, Oregon, Washington) | Business Development Executive

Sage is a senior-living technology platform that connects care management, staffing, and analytics to improve clinical and operational outcomes. The platform includes a caregiver-facing care app, EHR integrations, AI-powered data and recommendations for staffing and level-of-care decisions, analytics and reporting, and integrations with nurse-call and life-safety hardware. It is designed for use across the continuum of senior care (independent living, assisted living, memory care, and skilled nursing) to reduce response times and fall-related hospitalizations, improve caregiver satisfaction, and drive financial performance for communities.

You’ll be at the forefront of our sales team, driving revenue growth and building strong relationships with senior care communities across the country. You’ll work closely with our CEO, sales, and cross-functional teams to develop and execute a strategic sales plan, manage the full sales cycle, and close new business and expansion opportunities. This is a high-ownership role for someone who loves selling, thrives in a fast-moving environment, and wants to accelerate their craft quickly.

What You'll Do - Own the end-to-end sales process: prospecting, discovery, demos, pricing, negotiation, and close - Build and manage a strong pipeline within Assisted Living/Senior Living markets - Develop trusted relationships with decision-makers and senior stakeholders at target communities - Partner closely with the broader team to refine messaging, process, and go-to-market strategy - Bring a “challenge the status quo” mindset and help prospects understand the business impact of modernizing care delivery

Application details: We are committed to having a transparent and bias-free selection process that is inclusive to all applicants. To apply: https://www.sagehealth.com/company/careers#open-roles

author: DHFFounder
raw_html:
I'm building Diamond Hands Fantasy — think fantasy sports meets Wall Street. Users draft S&P 500, Nasdaq-100, and DJIA stocks into C-Suite roster positions (CEO, CFO, COO, etc.) and compete head-to-head in weekly matchups based on actual market performance.
Same skill-game legal framework as DraftKings/FanDuel. Incorporated Delaware C-Corp, fully spec'd, prototype built, GTM ready.
Looking for a CTO co-founder who can own the technical stack end-to-end. Stack is Next.js, React Native, Supabase, Postgres, Redis, BullMQ, Polygon.io for live market data.
Ideal background: fintech, gaming, or real-money platforms. Product ownership mindset over contractor mindset.
Equity: meaningful co-founder stake with standard vesting.
Based in NYC. Remote-friendly for the right person.
Reply here or email [diamondhandsfantasy@gmail.com].

author: rainbowsy
raw_html:
Rainbow Insurance (https://www.userainbow.com) | Backend Software Engineer | Full Time | Hybrid (2 days on-site in San Francisco) | Golang Rainbow is a tech-enabled small-business insurer. We built software that underwrites small businesses (starting with restaurants) and evaluates them against various risks to provide them with insurance policies. We've been live for only 2.5 years but already one of the top insurers of restaurants in the US. We are looking for an experienced software engineer to join our team and help us grow and innovate. We've raised around $20m to date with a series A around end of 2024. We've grown massively over the past year. Most of our problems we're currently working on are about building tech as we expand out to other categories of insurance and insuring other types of businesses.

Job Requirements:

- 5+ years of professional experience as a software engineer

- Experience in fintech is an optional but major plus

- Familiarity with our tech stack (Golang, React through a Next.js app, GraphQL, PostgreSQL) is beneficial but not required

Why You’d Enjoy Working with Us:

- You're joining a small fast paced team: 12 engineers, 40 people total

- Direct impact on solving real-world problems for small businesses

- Hybrid work model with 2 days on-site in SF

- Competitive salary and benefits

If interested, please reach out at hn@userainbow.com with your resume attached to the email.

You can also visit our site to learn more: https://www.userainbow.com/about

author: danielromerofi
raw_html:
VozParking | Founding Engineer / Technical Cofounder | Netherlands / Europe / REMOTE | Equity

VozParking is an early-stage mobility startup focused on helping drivers make faster, clearer parking decisions under pressure.

This is not meant to replace Google Maps or Waze. It’s a parking decision layer that sits on top of navigation tools, with voice as an important part of the interaction model to reduce phone use while driving.

Current stage:

early prototype exists core product logic is clear next milestone is a usable, testable MVP

Looking for:

a genuinely hands-on founding engineer / technical cofounder strong builder mindset comfortable across product + engineering interested in real-world interaction, systems, and usability challenges wants to build long-term, not freelance or advisory-only

If aligned, email: danielceo@vozparking.com Please include a short intro, what you’ve built, and how you like to work in early-stage products.

author: rdeadvisors
raw_html:
RDE Advisors | Senior Full-Stack Engineer | REMOTE (Europe, LatAm, India) | CONTRACT | $4K–6K USD/mo

We're a boutique commercial real estate advisory in NYC building two production web applications: a CRE search platform and a property management platform with QuickBooks integration. Both are live with real users. Stack is Next.js 14, TypeScript, Supabase/Postgres, Prisma, Anthropic Claude API, Mapbox, Vercel + Contabo.

Hiring one senior full-stack engineer to take both products from early-stage to production-mature. You'll work directly with the founder — no engineering managers, no PMs, no committees. 35–40 hrs/week, 6-month minimum, exclusive. Rate is $4,000–5,000 USD/mo for India-based engineers and $5,000–6,000 USD/mo for engineers in Europe or Latin America.

Requirements: 5+ years full-stack, 3+ shipping production web apps, strong Next.js/React + TypeScript, shipped at least one production LLM integration (Claude preferred, OpenAI fine), 2+ hours daily overlap with NYC, available for a 30-min video check-in every weekday at 9:30am ET. This is a product engineering role that calls the Claude API, not an ML/research role.

Non-negotiables: you personally write 100% of the code (no subcontracting, no "my team"), video on for all calls, personal verified GitHub, Hubstaff time tracking, ID check + one prior-client video reference before contract signing.

Apply: https://forms.office.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=bw3hQziC...

We respond to qualified applicants within one week. No DMs — please apply through the form.

author: connorb-coder
raw_html:
Coder | https://coder.com/ | Multiple roles | Multiple locations | Full-time

Coder is an AI software development company leading the future of autonomous coding. We empower teams to build software faster, more securely, and at scale through the collaboration of AI coding agents and human developers. Our mission is to make agentic AI a safe, trusted, and integral part of every software development lifecycle.

Coder’s self-hosted Agentic Development Environment (ADE) is the foundation for deploying agentic AI in the enterprise. It provides a secure, standardized, and governed workspace to deploy autonomous coding agents alongside human developers, accelerating innovation while maintaining control and compliance. Coder's isolated, policy-driven environments improve productivity, cut cloud costs, and reduce data risks. Developers transition to AI at their own pace using their own tools. Platform and security teams can govern, audit, and manage a great developer experience at scale.

We passed 100k stars on GitHub! https://github.com/coder

All positions are fully remote in the countries listed unless otherwise specified.

[1] Senior Software Engineer, Core Platform (back-end Go) - US/Canada

[2] Software Engineer, Core Platform (back-end Go) - US/Canada

[3] Software Engineering Manager - UK/Ireland/Poland

[4] Product Designer - US East Coast/UK/Ireland/Poland

And more! Apply here + job descriptions, salary bands: https://coder.com/careers?utm_source=O2n5Ew72WD

Not all of our jobs are listed here. Please note that you must submit an application to be considered for any of our positions. If you have any questions, please reach out to careers@coder.com.

raw_html:
Twindo (Formerly Canvas; The Spatial Computing Company) | Full-time | Remote

At Twindo, we’re working on spatial computing - using computer vision to 3D reconstruct and understand your surroundings so that software can operate over real-world spaces.

Twindo brings together everything that professionals need to understand the spaces they design, build, and manage, without extra site visits or second-guessing. With a quick scan or plan upload, any space becomes structured, accurate, design-ready data your whole team can actually use.

- Backend Engineer (Europe or LatAm Remote) - Full Stack Engineer (Europe or LatAm Remote) - iOS & Backend Engineer (Europe or LatAm Remote)

For detailed information check out https://www.twindo.com/careers/ or email jobs@twindo.com

Keywords: 3D, computer vision, photogrammetry, geomatics, SLAM, CAD, iOS, Python, Django

author: neonnomad
raw_html:
b.well | Sr SRE | US - Remote | bwell.com | Salary: $154-190,000 DOE

https://www.icanbwell.com/careers/?gnk=job&gni=8a78859e9cd55...

Company: We are the Healthtech powering brands like Google, Samsung, Perplexity, OpenAI, Noom and others. We are empowering people to own their health data and to drive detailed insights so they can make more informed decisions.

Stack: AWS, EKS (Bottlerocket), Alpine Imagges, Groundcover (Obersvability)

Job Description

As a Senior Site Reliability Engineer you will be tasked with making sure we build a reliable, secure and efficient platform for the b. Well network. You will be encouraged to blog, speak, and join events to talk about the work you are doing and encourage other companies to follow our lead. We will build solutions to delight our customers, employees and business. This role will also focus heavily on automation as it is a core value to our business from reliability to scaling.

What you will do:

-Let the robots do the work! We want you to focus on automating everything from building infrastructure, monitoring and disaster recovery

-Python, Rust, Go Go Go. Whatever you are comfortable in programming/scripting in we just want to see you deliver

-Love Penguins? We do too, whatever your flavor of Linux is bring your deep technical experience to the team

-New Platform, who dis? Build new solutions that enable our business and pass the knowledge on to your peers

-Like the Eye of Sauron you will monitor our infrastructure for anomalies and issues

-Code is for building solutions, not in how you communicate. Work well with your peers and communicate clearly so they understand the WHY behind what we do

-Must feed, water and provide high fidelity low friction platform solutions to engineers to keep them happy

-Write the ancient artifacts of documentation so your peers know how things work in the environment and write policies/procedures that make sense for the business

-Pass on your knowledge and wisdom to your other team members

-You like reading about the latest technology and trying it out? Come get paid for it!

author: smilingbuddhaa
raw_html:
Curie | AI Engineer | Hybrid (Chicago), Remote (San Francisco) | Full-time

Curie is a telehealth platform combining clinical expertise with AI to deliver personalized, accessible care — intelligent intake, treatment matching, and AI-powered tools for clinicians. Team includes ex-founders, clinicians, and engineers from Stanford, Harvard, UCLA, Berkeley, and AWS. Well-funded and growing fast.

We're hiring an AI Engineer to own the microservice layer powering our clinical AI: agentic intake workflows, RAG pipelines grounded in medical guidelines, session/memory management for long-running clinical agents, and HIPAA-grade observability and safety guardrails. You'll shape the AI architecture of a healthcare product from the ground up.

Stack: Langgraph, Langfuse, Go, Python, TS, GraphQL, gRPC/Connect-RPC, PostgreSQL, GCP/Vertex AI, AWS Bedrock.

Looking for: 5+ years engineering, meaningful production AI/ML experience, LLMs in production (prompting, structured output, evals), RAG and agentic patterns, comfort across Go + Python + cloud. Bonus: FHIR/HL7, fine-tuning, healthcare AI background, startup experience.

Apply: https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/curie/cea56a9a-d238-4101-a56c-f94a4...

author: wipaveeknecht
raw_html:
ChainSecurity | Blockchain Security Engineer | Full-time | INTERNS , HYBRID/REMOTE, Zurich, Switzerland | VISA possible | https://chainsecurity.com/

ChainSecurity is a young and innovative cybersecurity company that operates in the blockchain and cryptocurrency space. Our mission is to make the blockchain space secure and trustworthy for people, companies, and governments alike. We are proud of our security experts from the renowned universities ETH Zurich, EPFL Lausanne and others.

We are trusted by 100+ blockchain companies and established corporations with security audits and services. We have secured complex systems for top blockchain clients, such as decentralized finance (DeFi) projects, and multinational organizations, such as banks and industrial consortia. A ChainSecurity Auditor will review the most complex blockchain projects to find vulnerabilities which no-one else can. By using state of the art tools, expert knowledge and a deep understanding of the fundamentals, we secure projects handling billions, often forming the foundation in the new paradigm of an open financial system.

Your Responsibilities:

* Work directly with the developers of the most successful teams in the blockchain industry to audit their code and secure their products

* Contribute to our security tools and create new ones in collaboration with our internal research team

* Conduct security research and publish your findings Requirements:

* Strong background in software engineering, focused on security relevant areas and most importantly being able to reason about complex code

* Experience with penetration testing, vulnerability scanning, threat modeling or similar is a plus

* Good knowledge of cryptography

* Proficiency with Linux and Git

* Good written communication skills

* Fluent in OOP programming language

Bonus Points:

* Formal Verification, White Box, Vulnerability Scanning

* Solid Blockchain Knowledge

* Consensus Algorithms

Join us here: https://www.chainsecurity.com/jobs/blockchain-security-engin...

Questions about the role? Contact: jobs@chainsecurity.com

author: nj_builder_2026
raw_html:
Freight Brokerage | NJ | Founding Backend Architect (Python/PostgreSQL) | Remote (US, EST hours) | $150K + benefits

We're a freight brokerage in NJ building a self-hosted, AI-first platform to replace our legacy CRM/TMS.

The AI compute infrastructure and data foundation are already in place. We need the architect who turns it into a production operating system.

This is a systems architecture role, not a feature dev role. If your primary strength is writing features or following tickets, this is not the role.

You will be responsible for: designing and deploying a PostgreSQL schema for complex freight operations across 30+ entities. Building Python service layers integrating multiple REST APIs. Wiring locally-hosted LLM inference into business workflows. Structuring systems that involve document ingestion, email processing, and asynchronous event-driven pipelines. Defining backend patterns for reliability and maintainability across a multi-million record environment.

The entire stack is self-hosted on custom-built server hardware. Database, application layer, AI inference — all of it. No cloud dependencies. No API rate limits. No monthly GPU bills. No vendor lock-in. You have direct access to the metal, you deploy on your terms, and the only ceiling on what you can run is the hardware itself.

If you've ever wanted to build a production platform without a cloud provider standing between you and your infrastructure, this is that role.

You are the first technical hire. You report directly to the owner, who is directly involved in this hire and in daily collaboration on the build. Architectural decisions are yours to make. AI coding tools and low-code UI frameworks are part of the workflow — your time goes to data models, service orchestration, and API plumbing, not hand-building screens.

Stack: Python, PostgreSQL, FastAPI, Docker, Neo4j, Linux, vLLM. Preferred: freight/TMS domain, Microsoft Graph API, CRM migration experience.

1-week paid onsite onboarding in NJ, then fully remote. Equity conversation open once mutual fit is established.

To apply, send: (1) your resume, (2) a brief writeup of a system you designed end-to-end — not code, but the architecture decisions you made and why, (3) a short note on why this role interests you.

nj.technical.hire.spring.2026@proton.me

author: jdhawk
raw_html:
Results Generation | Cloud & DevOps Engineer | Austin, TX (Hybrid)

  We're a digital marketing platform company running a fleet of PHP/Lumen microservices on GCP, and supporting/rebuilding a legacy monolith. Small team, no bureaucracy, real infrastructure problems — multi-cluster Kubernetes, async messaging, database replication, content delivery at scale.
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  You'll own day-to-day infrastructure operations: multi-cluster Kubernetes, Terraform-driven GCP resources, Jenkins CI/CD pipelines, and on-call. Hands-on role — you're the person keeping production reliable and making it better.
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  Stack: GCP (GKE, Cloud SQL, GCS, Secret Manager, Workload Identity), Kubernetes, Terraform + Atlantis, Jenkins, Helm 3, Docker, MySQL (primary + replicas), RabbitMQ, Redis/Dragonfly, Elasticsearch, Grafana/Prometheus, Scalyr, Flux, Ansible.
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  Looking for: 3-5 years with cloud infrastructure in production. Solid Kubernetes and Terraform experience. Comfortable operating MySQL with replication. On-call experience — you've been paged and written postmortems. Networking fundamentals (VPNs, private endpoints, DNS, TLS).
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  Nice to have: GCP-specific experience, RabbitMQ, Elasticsearch, GitOps tooling, multi-cloud or cross-cloud migration experience.
https://www.indeed.com/job/cloud-devops-engineer-9234cb66e9a...

or just email me: dhawkins over at resultsgeneration.com

author: brandmultiplier
raw_html:
BrandMultiplier | Head of AI & Engineering | REMOTE | Fractional-to-Full-Time | Equity

We're a profitable B2B services company ($3M–$50M ARR clients) actively building a proprietary AI platform — and we need the technical leader to own the execution.

WHAT WE'RE BUILDING: A multi-agent AI platform that operationalizes our founder-story methodology into deployable client systems. Architecture spec exists, pieces are already in production. We're not starting from scratch — we're scaling a real build.

THE STACK: LLMs (Claude, GPT, Gemini), multi-agent orchestration (LangGraph/LangChain), RAG pipelines, vector databases, React/Next.js, Python/Node.js, AWS, n8n/Make for automation.

WHAT MATTERS MOST: You're not just an executor. You come to the leadership table with tools and approaches we haven't seen yet. You translate the CEO's vision into a technical spec, a build plan, and a shipped product — without needing everything pre-defined.

THE TEAM: 4 core people. You'd join the CEO, a Growth Strategist, and an Operations Manager — and lead one AI Engineer (your first direct report).

ENGAGEMENT: Starts fractional with a clear path to full-time and equity for the right fit. We're not VC-funded by design, so equity is unencumbered.

Read the full spec here: https://www.notion.so/brandmultiplier/Head-of-AI-Engineering...

author: deyahealth
raw_html:
Deya | Software Engineer (AI x Healthcare) | NYC (Hybrid) | Full-Time

Most AI in healthcare is being built in the wrong place.

Everyone is chasing scribes and diagnostics. Almost no one is building the longitudinal data layer, the system that captures what actually happens to patients over time.

That’s the real moat. We’re building it for eye care.

Deya is an AI platform that sits between doctor and patient, capturing symptoms, treatments, outcomes, and ocular images over time to enable continuous care.

We’re already piloting with eye doctors and collecting real patient data, including the largest dataset of eye images captured on smartphones.

What you’ll do:

- Build core product + infrastructure from 0→1

- Work across full stack, AI systems (LLMs + vision), and data pipelines

- Help define the architecture of a longitudinal healthcare data layer

Who you are:

- Full-stack engineer (backend / full stack; AI experience a plus)

- Wants ownership and to build from first principles

- Interested in healthcare, data, and real-world impact

Why this is interesting:

- Greenfield system with real-world data + distribution

- Direct access to clinical workflows and outcomes

- Small, high-ownership team

NYC-based, Hybrid.

Apply: https://deya.health/careers/software-engineer-ai-x-healthcar...

raw_html:
Chestnut | Product Engineers (Backend / Full-Stack) | SF + Seattle (Hybrid) | https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/Chestnut

Chestnut builds the systems insurance carriers actually run their distribution business on. We replace spreadsheets, legacy vendor systems, and manual processes with real-time data, deterministic workflows, and AI-assisted tooling.

This is end-to-end product engineering.

You won’t just write APIs. You’ll design schemas, build backend logic, ship UI, deploy to production, and handle the sharp edges that show up with real customers. The problems are messy and state-heavy: payouts that must reconcile with finance, onboarding flows with regulatory constraints, dynamic hierarchies, retries, chargebacks, partial failures.

We care a lot about correctness. These systems move real money and cannot drift.

Stack: Go, TypeScript/React, Postgres, Temporal, Kubernetes, AWS

We use AI heavily in our workflow — for drafting, refactoring, test generation, and exploring solutions. We expect engineers to use it aggressively. We also expect you to understand what it’s doing, spot when it’s wrong, and maintain strong fundamentals. Taste matters.

You’ll likely thrive here if you: - Have 3–6 years building real product systems - Are strong in at least one typed language and comfortable picking up others - Enjoy owning features end-to-end - Think in terms of invariants, edge cases, and failure modes - Like shipping quickly but cleanly - Want to build systems that operate in production, not demos

We’re a small, high-agency team. You’ll have meaningful ownership and direct impact on customers.

Hybrid in SF and Seattle.

Questions or want to sanity-check fit: nick [at] chestnutfi [dot] com

author: smede
raw_html:
AI engineer and Fullstack Engineer We are vysr GmbH, an early-stage startup on a mission to reinvent performance management for asset‑intensive industries. Using AI‑powered performance mining, we identify improvement potential, recommend high‑impact actions, and help organizations run asset management in a strategic, value‑driven way.

We're continuing building the platform to grow post MVP — and we’re looking for an engineers who want to build the application further

Your Responsibilities (AI engineer)

Develop, train, and optimize machine learning models for performance analysis, forecasting, and automated recommendations Build scalable AI services that integrate seamlessly with our cloud‑native backend Collaborate on the design of data pipelines and feature engineering processes Work with structured and unstructured data from enterprise systems and operational sources

Influence architectural decisions for our AI stack, from model serving to real‑time inference

Your Responsibilities (fullstack engineer) Build and scale frontend and backend systems from scratch Develop modern, responsive user interfaces using React, Tailwind CSS, or Material UI Implement REST and GraphQL APIs and integrate with complex enterprise systems such as SAP S/4HANA Design and maintain data pipelines and database architectures (e.g., PostgreSQL, Snowflake) Build real‑time features using WebSockets or Server‑Sent Events (SSE) Implement secure and scalable authentication and authorization (e.g., OAuth2, JWT, SSO)

Location: Onsite in Germany: Munich or Berlin --> please only apply if you are located in either of these cities Contact: steffen@vysr.io LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/in/steffen-medele

author: trustlesecurity
raw_html:
Trustle | Customer Success Engineer | Remote (US) | Full-time | Pre-Series A

We enforce Zero Standing Privileges across cloud and SaaS — AWS, GCP, Azure, Snowflake, Okta, GitHub, and more. Access is requested just-in-time and disappears when the job is done. We are a small team and AI is core to how we work.

This role sits between customers, the CEO, and engineering. You'll build real multi-cloud demo environments (not toy setups), run technical sales conversations, own customer onboarding, and create technical content grounded in actual depth. When you find a product problem, you'll file it and often see it fixed the same week.

You should have: Comfort scripting and hitting APIs to automate your own work Basic cloud familiarity (AWS, GCP, or Azure) — you know what a role and a service account are

Experience in customer-facing technical conversations A habit of publishing: blog, GitHub, videos, etc

Bonus: IAM/cloud security background, experience with agentic AI/LLM workflows, GCP stack (GKE, Cloud SQL, Cloud Run)

Pre-Series A means your work directly shapes product and GTM. Cloud access management is one of the fastest-growing problems in security — this is a good place to build expertise at the center of it. Interested? Contact us careers (at) trustle.com | https://trustle.com

author: kyry_cz
raw_html:
ThreatMark | Security, Machine Learning | REMOTE(World) / Brno, Czech Republic | Equity

ThreatMark protects >50M end users from finacial fraud and saves > $100M every year in losses. We are working with top banks in EU and US to protect their end users. We develop large-behavioral models that recognizes returning users by their mouse movements, keystroke timings and sensoric data (accelerometer, gyroscope).

We detect cyberthreats (e.g. malware, phishing, scraping) by exploiting browsers/sandboxes of devices. We manage ~PB scale of data and provide realtime scoring in milliseconds driving authentication/authorization flow (2nd factor).

Stack: [python, typescript, rust], [redis, postgresql], [aws, terraform] ...

We are looking for exceptional engineers to build with us and be part of the success:

- Backend Engineer [rust, python] - low-latency stream processing engine, rewrite python microservices to rust

- DevOps Engineer (IaaC in AWS)

If you are interested, please email us @ career@threatmark.com with subject "HN Who's Hiring"

If you don't find the right role here or at https://www.threatmark.com/careers/, write us anyway if you believe you can be good fit :)

Krystof Hilar, Co-Founder & CTO

author: raziborsky
raw_html:
Temper | Founding Engineer | SF (In-Person) | $150K–$250K + Equity | https://ontemper.com Every time two companies in the physical economy need to exchange data, someone builds a custom integration by hand. Analysts, engineers, QA. All manual. Every time. Google "EDI" if you haven't. It's wild.

We built AI agents that do the whole thing: gather requirements in plain English, write the integration code, test it, deploy it, fix it when it breaks.

We have paying enterprise customers and more demand than we can keep up with.

You'd be the first engineering hire. Agent orchestration, durable execution runtime, self-healing infra, most of the important architectural decisions haven't been made yet.

The founders built a logistics startup from pre-seed to 400+ people. We've been dealing with broken integrations for years.

TypeScript, Effect, Bun, PostgreSQL, K8s, AWS. Paid work day. Under 2 weeks to offer.

work@ontemper.com, mention HN

author: phm_robb
raw_html:
Project Healthy Minds (https://www.projecthealthyminds.com) | NYC (In-Office) | Full-Time

We're a mental health nonprofit that runs like a tech startup — building the world's first digital marketplace for finding mental health services. Aggregator UX simplicity, but for therapy, psychiatry, support groups, and other mental health services. Small team punching above its weight.

Stack: NestJS, NextJS, Postgres, on GCP. AI-assisted dev tooling and agentic workflows are a first-class part of our process. Hiring:

Lead Software Engineer — Design and build LLM-driven workflows to ingest, normalize, and maintain production-grade data from disparate sources. Define schemas, data contracts, and ranking/retrieval logic. ~10+ yrs, backend/data infra. $225K–$250K. https://jobs.lever.co/project-healthy-minds/514c5d68-8e18-45...

Product Engineer, Services — Build and evaluate LLM-enabled workflows for acquiring, structuring, and enriching services data. Collab with Lead. $155K–$195K. https://jobs.lever.co/project-healthy-minds/b132246f-0429-4d...

Product Engineer, User Journey — Own the frontend experience and analytics instrumentation (Segment, PostHog, GTM). Ship features end-to-end, measure impact, identify improvement opportunities from user testing and passive analytics. Collab with Product Manager and Product Designer. Fullstack with a frontend focus — comfortable across the stack but you care about what users see and feel. $155K–$195K. https://jobs.lever.co/project-healthy-minds/188ced8e-66b9-44...

Hiring Process: 30min Phone screen + 60 min in-person (talk about solving hard problems) + paid work trial to get to know the team.

https://jobs.lever.co/project-healthy-minds/188ced8e-66b9-44... (https://jobs.lever.co/project-healthy-minds/188ced8e-66b9-44c0-93f8-eb947df57005?lever-origin=applied&lever-source%5B%5D=Hacker%20News)

author: jaz46
raw_html:
Baseten Labs | San Francisco, New York |ONSITE, REMOTE, HYBRID | VISA SPONSORSHIP | RELOCATION SUPPORT

We are Baseten, the team that powers mission-critical inference for the world’s most dynamic AI companies such as Cursor, Notion, OpenEvidence, Abridge, Clay, Gamma and Writer. We’re growing quickly and recently raised our $300M Series E, backed by investors including BOND, IVP, Spark Capital, Greylock and Conviction. By uniting applied AI research, flexible infrastructure, and seamless developer tooling, we enable companies operating at the frontier of AI to bring cutting-edge models into production.

Apply Here: https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/baseten?utm_source=gkzEP29VD4 or email Jason.dupree@baseten.co

We are hiring for multiple core roles:

1. Solution Architect: You will partner closely with sales and customers to translate business needs into technical solutions, run technical discovery, and guide repeatable deployments and proofs of value for customers. This role is a great fit for entrepreneurial, customer-facing technical professionals who want a front-row view into how modern companies adopt AI at scale, and who enjoy working across technical discovery, solution design, demos, deployment scoping, and hands-on customer implementations, in close partnerships with Sales and Engineering

2. AI Solutions Engineer/Forward Deployed Engineer: You will partner directly with customers to architect, build, and deploy high-scale production AI applications on Baseten’s platform. You’ll own the journey with customers from initial exploration to production deployment. To be clear, this is a technical engineering role with hands-on coding and software dev that also includes aspects of product management, technical customer success and pre-sales solutions engineering mixed in. We’re language agnostic but strong Python and ML experience is a big plus.

3. Customer Engineer: You'll partner closely with Solutions Architecture, SRE, Infra, Product, and Forward Deployed Engineering, but you own the customer outcome end-to-end: from first response to root-cause analysis to the follow-up that reinforces trust. This role blends deep infrastructure debugging, AI/ML performance expertise, incident command, and proactive account ownership. You'll triage and resolve issues across Kubernetes, GPUs, networking, and model serving, lead war rooms during P0 escalations, and translate recurring pain points into product improvements.

The Offer:

Compensation: Very competitive, we’re looking for folks with a breadth of experience

Equity: This is an opportunity to participate in the ownership of a fast-growing AI Infrastructure platform

Location: We are based out of San Francisco with offices in New York. We are also open to remote employees for these roles.

Process: If you’re interested, please apply here https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/baseten?utm_source=gkzEP29VD4 or email me direct at Jason.dupree@baseten.co

author: rp_talent
raw_html:
Redpanda Data | REMOTE | Full-time | https://www.redpanda.com/careers

Redpanda is pioneering the Agentic Data Plane (ADP) - a new category in AI infrastructure that makes it simple and secure to connect AI agents with enterprise data and systems. Built on a multi-modal data streaming engine, Redpanda empowers agentic applications that reason and act in real-time with speed, autonomy, and precision. Global leaders including Activision Blizzard, Cisco, Moody's, Texas Instruments, Vodafone and 2 of the top 5 banks in the U.S. rely on Redpanda to process hundreds of terabytes of data a day. Backed by premier venture investors Lightspeed, GV and Haystack VC, Redpanda is a diverse, people-first organization with teams distributed around the globe.

Software Engineer, Core Enterprise - https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/redpandadata/jobs/465284300...

Software Engineer, Core SQL - https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/redpandadata/jobs/465929600...

author: msowin1
raw_html:
Instinct Science | Senior Software Engineer (React Native) | REMOTE (US) | Full-Time | $130K–$160K

Instinct Science is the AI leader in veterinary medicine. We build the industry's first clinical intelligence platform — combining a cloud-native EMR, AI-powered scribing (ScribbleVet), real-time clinical decision support, and Plumb's drug information — in one system that helps veterinary teams work smarter, not harder. We acquired ScribbleVet in early 2026 and are building out its next chapter.

This role is embedded directly in the ScribbleVet product team. You'll own the mobile app used daily by thousands of veterinarians to automate clinical documentation.

WHAT YOU'LL DO: - Build and own cross-platform features end-to-end in React Native (iOS + Android) - Identify and resolve performance bottlenecks — state management, native bridges, CI/CD - Ship real AI-powered features alongside a small, high-output team - Collaborate closely with design, product, and backend engineers

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR: - Deep React Native expertise with production mobile app experience - Strong product instincts and QA discipline — you care about what you ship - Actively using AI coding tools

STACK: React Native, Expo, TypeScript, GraphQL

Apply: https://instinctscience.applytojob.com/apply/4Yj4DavJ6D/Seni...

author: wrangelvid
raw_html:
Evolve Robotics | XR Engineer, Full-Stack Engineer, Real-Time Engineer | San Francisco ONSITE | $140-200k + equity | https://EvolveRobotics.com

We let people embody machines from anywhere on the planet. Humanoids, drones, industrial arms -- if it moves, someone in a headset can be inside it, doing real work. You know how people remote into desktops? We do that, but for the physical world.

Team of 5 scaling to 10.

XR Engineer -- Build the interface between human input and robot movement. Hand/body tracking, gaze, controllers, spatial UI. Make teleoperation feel like teleportation, not a laggy video call. Experience with three.js, react-three/fiber and the pmndrs ecosystem is a big plus.

Full-Stack Engineer -- Product verticals end-to-end: data modeling, server routes, UI, real-time sync. Fleet management, operator scheduling, org tooling. TypeScript/React. Experience with sync engines or local-first architectures is a big plus.

Real-Time Engineer -- WebRTC internals, peer-to-peer data channels + video, connection lifecycle management in Go. Sub-frame latency with real consequences -- these are physical machines. If you've worked on real-time media infrastructure and want to apply it to something that actually moves, this is it.

We don't care about your title. We care that you can own a problem space end-to-end, move independently, and ship things that matter.

To apply: go to https://EvolveRobotics.com, find the contact form, and beat the game. If you can't get past Evo, this might not be the role.

author: maijobs
raw_html:
Microsoft AI | Platform Engineering | Full-time | Greater Seattle Area, WA or Bay Area (Mountain View), CA | ONSITE (3 days/week) We're building the platform primitives that power https://copilot.com (Microsoft's ChatGPT). If you've shipped systems at scale—whether backend services, distributed infrastructure, ML platforms, or consumer AI products—we want to talk to you.

The work: design and build secure, performant platform services for AI at scale; collaborate with infrastructure, product, design, and research teams; ship high-quality code that reaches hundreds of millions of users; move fast in a design-driven environment.

You: solid fundamentals in systems thinking and software engineering; fluent in Python, C#, C++, Java, JavaScript, or similar; shipped real products or systems people use; can communicate across technical and non-technical teams. Worked at companies/products of global scale and at least 1 startup-like environment (startup or internal incubator).

Compensation: Competitive with other frontier AI companies, commensurate with experience. Relocation available.

We're actively hiring and will respond to qualified applicants. Check https://microsoft.ai/careers for current openings, or email maiplatformjobs@microsoft.com. Apply directly if genuinely interested in building the future of AI.

New grads: If you've had 2+ MAG7+ internships (or adjacent large scale experience) AND a strong transcript from your university. Use [NEW GRAD] in the subject line.

author: asoto
raw_html:
Trace Labs | Remote (USA) | Full Time | https://tracelabs.ai/ Want to join a lean, ambitious, and fast growing startup?

Trace is building the data marketplace for physical AI. Robotics and embodied AI are bottlenecked by real-world data. We’re building the platform that turns real-world human work into structured training datasets for robotics and foundation model labs. We’re hiring engineers to help build the core product from end to end.

Small, senior team. Fully remote. Experienced founders with a successful exit. Clear communicators. High trust, high bar.

Open Roles

- Full Stack Software Engineer: https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/tracelabs/963005ce-f4f1-4d54-957d-1...

- Backend Software Engineer: https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/tracelabs/a80546f7-6253-4dfa-95be-c...

What We Offer

- Autonomy, impact, and ownership. As an early employee you’ll shape the product direction and company values.

- Competitive salary and significant equity in a fast growing, early stage startup.

- Remote employment - work where you want, when you want.

- Generous vacation policy - take time whenever you need to recharge. Building a lasting company is a marathon, not a sprint.

Given the latest quantum algorithmic advances by Google, Bitcoin is expected to be fully broken by 2029. In this position you will design post-quantum cryptography that goes beyond the BIP-360 stopgap to fully solve the quantum threat on Bitcoin and transition it safely into the post quantum age.

Please describe your cryptographic experience, your Bitcoin experience and the flavor of PQ-crypto that you believe can save BTC. Apply to: savingbitcoin@pindush.net

(burner address for spam prevention, expect answers from a different email)

raw_html:
PermitFlow (YC W22) | Staff, Fullstack & Frontend Software Engineers | New York, NY | ONSITE | Full-time | https://permitflow.com PermitFlow’s mission is to streamline and simplify construction permitting in the $1.6 trillion United States construction market. Our software reduces time to permit, supporting permitting end-to-end including permit research, application preparation, submission, and monitoring.

We recently raised a $54M Series B led by Accel with participation from Kleiner Perkins, Felicis, Altos Ventures, and YC.

Permitting is a challenging yet interesting problem; thousands of municipalities across the United States have their own processes and requirements - many which are continually updating. Software, AI, and LLMs provide an exciting opportunity for us to reason at scale and distill a nuanced and localized permitting process into a standard and streamlined experience for our customers.

We're looking for customer-obsessed, ambitious, and hard-working teammates who are excited about solving a real-world problem with technology at its helm.

NYC Onsite Roles | apply at: https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/permitflow?departmentId=d33195eb-89...

1. Staff Software Engineer - $200K-275K + equity

2. Fullstack Software Engineer - $125-245K + equity

3. Senior Frontend Engineer - $125K-245K + equity

https://permitflow.com/hiring

author: u920705
raw_html:
Aye Eye is building a privacy-first edge-AI camera platform: local AI, live video, device intelligence, and the app / developer tools around it.

We’re hiring for 3 roles Remote (UK/EU preferred):

1) Senior Product Designer Own UX/UI for the Aye Eye / Trojan app platform. Mobile/tablet journeys, wireframes, Figma design system, and clickable prototypes. First mission includes onboarding, dashboard, live view, alerts, device setup, AI query, shop, subscription, and settings.

2) Developer Platform Lead Define the self-serve developer ecosystem: portal structure, onboarding flows, documentation, sandbox model, and submission workflows.

3) Senior Full Stack Developer Build the portal behind that ecosystem: auth, dashboards, API keys, sandbox access, submission workflows, and licensing touchpoints.

We want people who can simplify complex systems, work fast, and ship clean, commercially usable product experiences.

Email: ayeeye.careers@gmail.com Subject: HN – [Role Name]

author: camwardy
raw_html:
Adclear | Senior Fullstack Engineer | London, UK | HYBRID (4 days/week) | Full-Time

We're turning compliance from a bottleneck into a creative advantage for regulated brands. Clients include Monzo, Lloyds, PensionBee, and Plum. Raised £2.6m+ from Outward VC in our first 14 months.

The problem: we run multi-agent AI pipelines that evaluate regulated marketing content in real time. The model landscape moves fast and our architecture has to move with it. You'd own how these systems evolve in production, working directly with the CTO and AI engineers, while also shipping across the full stack.

Stack: TypeScript, React, React Router v7, Tailwind, shadcn/ui | Hono on Cloudflare Workers, Drizzle ORM, PostgreSQL (Neon) | Python, FastAPI, LangChain + LangGraph | GitHub Actions

We're 6 engineers. You'd have real input into architecture, tooling, and how the team operates. We use Cursor, Claude Code and Greptile day-to-day.

£75-95k + equity. Private healthcare, gym budget, hardware budget.

Apply (mention "Hacker News" in your notice period section): https://www.adclear.ai/jobs-seniorengineer

author: cschalmers
raw_html:
CS PhD position | Chalmers University | Sweden | ONSITE | machine learning for biology

At CGR Lab (https://cgrlab.github.io/research/) based in Chalmers, Gothenburg, we work at the intersection of biology and machine learning.

* develop deep learning architectures for biology

* transformer-based models (eg BERT) and state space models (eg Mamba) for large-scale biological data.

* build models to predict the functional effects of genomic variants

* no biology background required.

Email your CV to csphdchalmers@gmail.com

author: agastalver
raw_html:
Ploid AI | Barcelona, Spain | REMOTE (preferably hybrid) | Core Team | Full-time

We're building an AI-first bioinformatics platform to empower life science researchers with agentic AI and LLMs. Backed by pre-seed funding, a government grant, and customers from biotech, agrotech, and pharma companies, we're looking for a full-stack engineer to join as a core member.

What we're looking for:

• Strong full-stack experience across Go, TypeScript, and Python (Rust is a plus)

• Hands-on AWS infrastructure work (you've built and maintained production cloud environments)

• Experience with multi-tenant architectures: isolation strategies, auth, data partitioning

• Comfort with AI/ML devops: deploying and orchestrating model-serving pipelines, managing GPU workloads, CI/CD for ML systems

• Willingness to own problems end-to-end, from system design to shipping

Nice-to-haves (not all required): LLM integration, RAG systems, HPC infra, cloud/on-prem hybrid systems

About us: We're a team of engineers and scientists with strong academic and industry backgrounds (ex-BCG, PhD in Plant Biotech and Bioinformatics, Mathematics, M.Sc. AI, PhD in Electrical Engineering). We're committed to building something impactful, technical, and genuinely useful for life science.

Apply: hello@ploid.ai

More about us: https://ploid.ai

We also consider internship requests from strong student candidates for research work.

Thanks to everyone who's reached out through previous HN posts, this community has helped us find amazing people.

author: foundryrobotics
raw_html:
Foundry Robotics | Founding Engineers | Full-time | Onsite (London, UK)

I'm Gio, CTO of Foundry Robotics. We're an early stage startup building robotics for manufacturing applications.

We are looking for people with one or more of these skills:

1) Tinkerer/mechanical engineer -- Build robots in our lab with a mix of design and hands-on prototyping. Experience with metal fabrication (CAD, CNC milling, 3d printing, sheet metal).

2) Computer vision AI lead -- You will own our vision stack. Experience in object detection and pose estimation, SLAM, FoundationPose, Nvblox etc.

3) Robotics software engineer -- Build our manipulation pipeline. Experience in Nvidia Isaac or similar simulation environments.

Contact: gio@foundryrobotics.co.uk (start subject with "HN:")

Senior engineer to step into a ~30k LOC system (~300 files) and help shape it properly.

author: acsbiolm
raw_html:
BioLM | Lead Platform Engineer | San Francisco (or Remote) | Full-time | biolm.ai

We're building an AI platform for molecular discovery: think language models for protein and DNA design. Small founding team, moving fast, real ownership.

Looking for a lead platform engineer to own full-stack development across our Console, Builder, and Solutions apps. You'll ship agentic workflows for molecular design, build integrations with sequence/assay ordering providers, and help shape the technical direction of the platform.

Stack: Python, React, Node.js, AWS, Docker, REST/WebSockets. Bonus if you have experience with agentic systems or MCP. Interest in biology/bioinformatics a plus but not required. If you've shipped production systems end-to-end, have strong product instincts, and want to work on something at the intersection of AI and life sciences - we'd love to hear from you.

https://app.dover.com/apply/BioLM/12435b30-67f1-43c2-8c59-6d...

author: gpresearch2025
raw_html:
Pinehurst, TX | Remote, Onsite, Hybrid Senior Systems Architect (Backend / AI-Native / Not a CRUD Role)

Overview We are looking for a senior systems architect, not a feature developer. This role is for someone who: . has designed systems end-to-end . understands how to structure complex backend systems . can work alongside AI-assisted development (not against it) If your primary strength is writing features or following tickets, this is not the role.

What You Will Be Responsible For Evaluating existing systems and identifying what should be: kept, restructured, rebuilt Designing system architecture across: data flow, service boundaries, processing layers Structuring systems that involve: multiple data inputs (APIs, documents, etc.), document ingestion and parsing, asynchronous/background processing Defining backend patterns for: reliability, scalability, maintainability Working in an environment where AI tools (e.g. Claude, OpenAI Codex) are actively used for execution This is a design-first role, not a ticket-driven role.

How to Apply (Required) Please include: . A brief description of a system you personally designed end-to-end . The hardest architectural decision you made in that system . A short explanation of how you currently use AI tools in your workflow Applications without this information will not be considered.

Please email to Gpresearch2025@gmail.com

Due to the length of the comments, please visit this doc for full details: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1cCh3gjmq3QYrTKl4qv93QgD1...
raw_text:
Pinehurst, TX | Remote, Onsite, Hybrid
Senior Systems Architect (Backend / AI-Native / Not a CRUD Role)

Overview
We are looking for a senior systems architect, not a feature developer.
This role is for someone who:
. has designed systems end-to-end 
. understands how to structure complex backend systems 
. can work alongside AI-assisted development (not against it)
If your primary strength is writing features or following tickets, this is not the role.

What You Will Be Responsible For
Evaluating existing systems and identifying what should be: kept, restructured, rebuilt
Designing system architecture across: data flow, service boundaries, processing layers
Structuring systems that involve: multiple data inputs (APIs, documents, etc.), document ingestion and parsing, asynchronous/background processing
Defining backend patterns for: reliability, scalability, maintainability
Working in an environment where AI tools (e.g. Claude, OpenAI Codex) are actively used for execution
This is a design-first role, not a ticket-driven role.

How to Apply (Required)
Please include:
. A brief description of a system you personally designed end-to-end
. The hardest architectural decision you made in that system
. A short explanation of how you currently use AI tools in your workflow
Applications without this information will not be considered.

Please email to Gpresearch2025@gmail.com

Due to the length of the comments, please visit this doc for full details:

author: nfriedly
raw_html:
Fullstory | Atlanta, USA & Bogotá, Colombia | https://www.fullstory.com/

Fullstory offers privacy-preserving session replay and analytics for websites and mobile apps. Our session replays are nothing short of magical, and the combination with our automatic analytics creates eye-opening insights.

Atlanta, US engineering roles (semi-onsite, requiring 1 day per week in office):

* Senior Software Engineer, Web Capture - $145~170k + up to 10% bonus - JavaScript/TypeScript, React, Go (golang) - https://www.fullstory.com/careers/jobs/76e583a9-74c0-42d4-ae...

* Senior Software Engineer, Data Pipeline - $160~180k USD + up to 10% bonus - Go (golang), Kubernetes (K8s), GCP - https://www.fullstory.com/careers/jobs/a786962d-68c1-48fa-8b...

Bogotá, Colombia:

* Senior Software Engineer II, Guides & Surveys - $90~93k USD + up to 10% bonus - https://www.fullstory.com/careers/jobs/073bfc41-0ac6-4e59-a5...

We have additional roles for sales, consulting, HR, accounting, and customer support open in Atlanta, London, and some Remote (US) options. See the full list at https://www.fullstory.com/careers?utm_source=092xpqyAkV

For US roles, you can expect regular raises & bonuses, equity, and benefits including: health insurance, 401k matching (Vanguard), annual learning stipend, unlimited PTO, parental leave, and more. I take about 4-5 weeks vacation a year, in addition to ~3 weeks worth of company holidays (federal holidays + the week of Christmas to New Years.)

Folks in other countries can also expect a strong benefits package, but I'm not as versed on the details.

Additionally, you get a 5-week sabbatical after 5 years of employment - I took mine about a year ago, and it was fantastic.

To apply, please submit your info on the website, and our recruiting team will get in touch with you. If you have questions, feel free to reply here or contact me directly. (I'm an engineer on the iOS team, but I'll do my best to answer questions.)

* Senior Software Engineer II, Guides & Surveys - $90~93k USD + up to 10% bonus - https://www.fullstory.com/careers/jobs/073bfc41-0ac6-4e59-a5... (https://www.fullstory.com/careers/jobs/073bfc41-0ac6-4e59-a587-2536c2cd26c4/?ashby_jid=073bfc41-0ac6-4e59-a587-2536c2cd26c4&utm_source=092xpqyAkV)

We have additional roles for sales, consulting, HR, accounting, and customer support open in Atlanta, London, and some Remote (US) options. See the full list at https://www.fullstory.com/careers?utm_source=092xpqyAkV

For US roles, you can expect regular raises & bonuses, equity, and benefits including: health insurance, 401k matching (Vanguard), annual learning stipend, unlimited PTO, parental leave, and more. I take about 4-5 weeks vacation a year, in addition to ~3 weeks worth of company holidays (federal holidays + the week of Christmas to New Years.)

Folks in other countries can also expect a strong benefits package, but I'm not as versed on the details.

Additionally, you get a 5-week sabbatical after 5 years of employment - I took mine about a year ago, and it was fantastic.

To apply, please submit your info on the website, and our recruiting team will get in touch with you. If you have questions, feel free to reply here or contact me directly. (I'm an engineer on the iOS team, but I'll do my best to answer questions.)

author: onoshealth
raw_html:
Onos Health | Staff Eng (AI), Lead Eng (Backend), Lead Eng (Data) | San Francisco (Hybrid) | $200-250k + Equity | [https://onoshealth.com](https://onoshealth.com/)

30 cents of every behavioral health dollar in the U.S. is wasted — on ineffective care, administrative overhead, and outright fraud. Onos fixes this by ensuring every healthcare dollar goes toward high quality care.

Our AI platform helps health plans identify where care is going wrong, get members on the right clinical pathway, catch fraud and abuse, and automate reviews that clinical teams currently do by hand. We're well funded by top healthcare investors and live with some of the nation's largest health plans.

Stack: Python/Django, Next.js/TypeScript, PostgreSQL, AWS (Bedrock, ECS, Glue, S3). We ship with Claude Code and CodeRabbitAI.

Hiring:

- Staff Software Engineer (AI) — LLM pipelines for clinical note extraction, level-of-care classification, eval frameworks - Lead Engineer (Backend) — clinical workflow engine, AI/ML pipeline integration, multi-tenant architecture - Lead Engineer (Data/Integrations) — payer data pipelines, EDI X12/FHIR/HL7, claims feed infrastructure

All roles: https://onoshealth.com/career Email: [joinus@onoshealth.com](mailto:joinus@onoshealth.com) (mention HN)

author: enayetn
raw_html:
Hey HN! I'm hiring aggressively to scale our team at Air Space Intelligence (https://www.airspace-intelligence.com)

Series B, onsite in Boston (all levels) and Seattle (Senior+).

We're building the software layer for airspace and critical infrastructure operations. Think real-time optimization across thousands of flights, grid assets, and logistics networks — the kind of problem where your state space is enormous, constraints shift constantly, and the humans in the loop are making life-safety decisions on tooling that hasn't meaningfully improved in decades.

We're hiring across Product, Infrastructure, and ML. If you're a performance minded front-end (web) engineer - I particularly would love to hear from you.

Here's one of our roles https://www.airspace-intelligence.com/careers/role?=6462d3d1... - there's a puzzle at the bottom that'll be trivial for this crowd, but it'll get you my email directly. If you go that route, drop "HackerNews" in the subject so I can prioritize you.

If building software for critical systems sounds interesting, I'd love to hear from you.

author: IanWoodfill
raw_html:
Hey all, Cassidy (https://cassidyai.com, Series A, ONSITE: New York City) is growing fast and we're hiring across a few critical roles in NYC.

Senior & Mid-level Software Engineers - end-to-end product engineering and helping build systems that scale

Technical Chief of Staff - CTO's operational right hand. Picks up hard, ambiguous problems across engineering, compliance, and commercial conversations and owns them end to end.

Cassidy is an AI agent and automation platform for non-technical knowledge workers. By connecting to a company's knowledge where it lives and building intuitive core primitives, Cassidy lets non-technical teams automate large swaths of busy work, freeing them up to do the work that really matters. Series A, 25 people, growing fast.

raw_html:
GTM Engineer | TestGorilla

TYPE: Full-time

LOCATION: Remote - Spain, UK via EOR, the rest between UTC +2 to UTC +5:30 in our hiring countries via contract (https://testgorilla-1680540968.teamtailor.com/pages/hiring-a...)

REMOTE: Yes (async-first)

VISA: No

DESCRIPTION: TestGorilla is hiring a GTM Engineer to build and scale an AI-enabled GTM system across Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success.

This is not a traditional RevOps or Analytics role. You’ll design and implement workflows, automations, and integrations that make GTM strategy execute reliably at scale.

Build & maintain GTM workflows (HubSpot, Gong, integrations) Automate routing, alerts, deal rules, and inspections Translate revenue strategy into system logic Connect product, sales, and customer data Enable AI-driven GTM signals (e.g. deal risk, expansion) Reduce manual ops work through automation

You should have experience with GTM/RevOps systems and be comfortable working with APIs, integrations, and data flows. Systems thinking and ability to work from ambiguity are key. Interest in AI-driven workflows is a plus.

COMPENSATION: €54,840–€68,550 + SARs (equity)

JD: https://testgorilla-1680540968.teamtailor.com/jobs/7143357-g...

author: mwapinska
raw_html:
Senior Python Backend Engineer | REMOTE (EMEA/APAC)

We’re looking for Python backend engineers to work on a trustless supercluster of performance-proofed GPU-enabled sandboxed docker container runners controlled by truly decentralized algorithms (not just PAXOS or RAFT).

Wow, that was a mouthful...

If you found it interesting, join Reef Technologies and tackle complex technical challenges like this on your own terms:

Contribute from wherever you like—we are fully remote Set your own time commitment, as long as it’s at least 30h per week See how we work in our handbook: github.com/reef-technologies/handbook Influence the way we operate through our Sociocracy 3.0 decision-making process

Salary on a B2B contract:

45-70 USD or 180-280 PLN per hour Flexible work schedule (as measured with a time tracker) We automatically adjust rates based on inflation twice a year

We're looking for someone with 5+ years of programming experience, including at least a year with Python (including opensource and significant personal projects).

So, what do you think? You don't even need a CV; just go to https://careers.reef.pl/?utm_source=hackernews and shoot a few quick commands to apply.

Contact: hiring@reef.pl Web: https://reef-technologies.com/

author: nossyah
raw_html:
Post Up | Full time | Senior Backend Engineer | Onsite | Vancouver, BC (Hybrid) | Salary: $120-$130k CAD | https://www.postup.pro

We're building the Databricks for content production, starting with post. Our orchestration engine coordinates pipelines end to end and turns post-production workflows into a persistent graph of assets and transformations, which are deterministic, verifiable, and nearly instant at scale, powered by a high-performance compute layer. We're building this AI-native from the ground up; ML inference is embedded throughout the pipeline, not bolted on.

Stack: On-prem, HPC clusters, distributed storage systems, GPU pipelines, real-time video compute.

We're looking for our founding senior backend engineer who wants to work on genuinely challenging infrastructure problems across distributed systems and media processing with immense scale. Should be familiar with Golang, Postgres, Distributed systems, Distributed filesystems (e.g. HDFS, Ceph, etc). If that sounds interesting, reach out directly to me at wilson@postup.pro.

Me: https://www.linkedin.com/in/hayswilson/

How to apply: Email me directly at wilson@postup.pro or Submit here - https://www.notion.so/Founding-Senior-Backend-Engineer-31031...

author: Freeformcareers
raw_html:
Freeform (http://freeform.co) | Software Engineers | Full-time | Onsite | Hawthorne, CA (Los Angeles County)

Freeform builds AI-native manufacturing systems that unify software, hardware, and physics to produce industrial-scale parts at the speed of human ideation. By treating manufacturing as a single integrated system, we unlock a new era of innovation where complex hardware is designed, built, and scaled without limits. This isn’t just an improvement; it’s a leap forward in how complex metal parts are made, with the ability to revolutionize industries like aerospace, defense, automotive, energy, and beyond. We are backed by top VCs including NVIDIA’s NVentures, Founders Fund, Threshold Ventures, and Apandion.

It’s a small, tight-knit team and you’d have the opportunity to own significant and impactful greenfield projects throughout the entire development lifecycle and much more. Our software team comes from the likes of Google, SpaceX, and Apple, and believes in taking a first principles approach to solving hard problems.

A glimpse into our software landscape - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nFWQArUkd5I

Our feature in TechCrunch announcing our $67M Series B Raise! https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/19/freeform-raises-67m-series...

Reqs will vary depending on what the hiring needs are but should be formatted as:

+ Principal Software Engineer - https://grnh.se/27ajbj9k3us

+ Senior Software Engineer - https://grnh.se/m2l08z853us

+ Senior Software Engineer (Embedded) - https://grnh.se/hb838bth3us

+ Senior Software Engineer, Factory Intelligence (Skyfall) - https://grnh.se/lq96qci53us

+ Senior Software Engineer (Edge Data & Telemetry) - https://grnh.se/h9i6f5da3us

+ Software Engineer (Edge Data & Telemetry) - https://grnh.se/0zmdu30j3us

author: jvachal
raw_html:
Antioch | Simulation Engineer | New York City | Full Time | https://antioch.com

Antioch is building the simulation platform for robotics and perception systems. Spin up full-stack digital twins, run scenarios to test and validate behavior, and deploy, entirely in software.

Role:

Build high-fidelity models of sensors and physical components:

— Design and implement simulation capabilities for sensors, actuators, and interactivity (contacts, dynamics, timing, noise, artifacts, etc.).

— Build and calibrate simulations for one or more sensing modalities (e.g. cameras, lidar, radar, infrared/thermal, UV), including realistic failure modes and edge cases.

- Model the full sensor pipeline where relevant: optics/RF + noise sources + signal processing (e.g. camera ISP behaviors, radar DSP stages).

Partner directly with customers to ingest their existing physical systems into Antioch (robots, sensors, actuators, environments):

— Translate incomplete specs + messy real-world data into working simulation models with clear assumptions and documented gaps.

— Drive the integration to completion: “runs in sim, matches reality within defined tolerances, and is usable by the customer.”

— Design and implement simulation capabilities for sensors, actuators, and interactivity (contacts, dynamics, timing, noise, artifacts, etc.).

— Build and calibrate simulations for one or more sensing modalities (e.g. cameras, lidar, radar, infrared/thermal, UV), including realistic failure modes and edge cases.

How to apply:

Submit an application to https://antioch.com/careers/38387

OR

Email Jackson at jackson@antioch.com with your resume and answers to the following:

- Please explain what robotics simulation experience you have had (i.e. Isaac Sim, MuJoCo, Pybullet, Gazebo, etc.)

- Please explain what sensor simulation experience you have had (Radar, Camera, LiDAR, Infrared, etc.)

- Please explain what excites you about this role!

author: rdh_hiring
raw_html:
Data Analyst | Redistricting Data Hub | Remote (US)

The nonpartisan Redistricting Data Hub provides individuals, good government organizations, and community groups the data, resources, and knowledge to participate effectively in the redistricting process. In service of this mission, we host thousands of datasets in all 50 states, and continue to add new data that is useful for map drawing and analysis.

We are currently hiring a full-time data analyst to join our team. In this role, you will work with geospatial data, election results, and other key datasets. We're seeking candidates who are comfortable working remotely on a small team, skilled in data processing and analysis, and experienced with Python.

Applications will be accepted through Friday, May 1, 2026. Visit our website for the full job description, including responsibilities and application instructions: https://redistrictingdatahub.org/join-us/work-with-us/

author: PoslavskySV
raw_html:
MiLaboratories | Bilbao, Spain | ONSITE | RELOCATION SUPPORT | https://platforma.bio

We are MiLaboratories, the team behind Platforma.bio & MiXCR. We are building the "OS for Biology"—the foundational infrastructure where all biological data is transformed into life-saving breakthroughs. We work fully on-site in Bilbao because the complexity of our problems (high-performance computing + deep biological nuances) requires high-bandwidth, in-person collaboration.

We dissolved traditional departmental silos. We are hiring hardcore builders, not analysts, for a critical core role:

*Senior TypeScript (SDK & Platform):* You will architect our frictionless, AI-native SDK to scale our ecosystem. This is not a CRUD web dashboard. You will build a heavy local desktop application that bridges directly with a high-performance Rust data layer. You must treat the browser engine as a runtime for heavy local apps. The standard is simple: if the SDK is too messy for us to build blocks at AI-speed, it is too messy for the world.

The Offer: * Comp: Top-tier EU salaries (€90k - €120k EUR). * Tax Advantage: Bizkaia has independent tax autonomy. You qualify for the "Special Impatriate Regime" (30% salary tax exemption + expense deductions - up to 50% in total). * The Reality: Your effective tax rate drops to ~15-20%, yielding a significantly higher net income than London or Berlin. * Stock: We grant ESOP and treat equity as a life-changing wealth vehicle, not a "nice to have." * Relocation: Comprehensive "Soft Landing" package (Visa, temporary housing, school search). * Location: Bilbao is a hidden gem—ocean, mountains, and high quality of life with a low cost of living.

Process: No HR screening. You will talk directly to the engineering team.

Email me (I am the CEO): stanp [at] milaboratories [dot] com. Please mention "HN" in the subject and include your GitHub.

author: drink_milk
raw_html:
SPADE Agriculture| Full Stack Engineer | https://spade.ag | Remote (US Timezones) | Full Time

Spade is a Series A startup building a wide range of products for the dairy industry.

Think apps for farm data, machine vision for cows and next generation embedded microchips for animal health data (its not evil I promise).

We are building tools with data and AI to create Superhuman Management for both the supply chain ( we have a enterprise suite for the companies that buy and process milk ) and farms.

Our customers are the biggest food companies and biggest dairy farms on the planet.

We are looking for people who love agentic coding, are willing to own whole applications, and want to work with an application that works with real-world data from real-world farms.

Never heard of a Ralph loop? NGMI...

Our best developers have < 2 hrs of meetings a week, we aim to make that 1 hr.

We want to push the limits of what what low bureaucracy, high autonomy building can look like.

We have up to quarterly in person meetups depending on your location.

Good pay and benefits obviously.

Formal Job Post https://drive.google.com/file/d/1nOLxCZJuKNJAOvopil4dxUTppuR...

Apply Here From Hacker News ( not the email in formal post ): https://airtable.com/appgtIW1XInxyXKvn/pagtPpYqGVai0d0lZ/for...

author: sm123
raw_html:
Founding Technical AE | https://build.io | REMOTE (working hrs must overlap w/ N America) | Full-time

Build is a full-stack cloud platform — we own the stack from bare metal up. Our customers are engineering-led SaaS teams who've outgrown their current setup and want effortless deployment, real environments, and predictable pricing without the infrastructure overhead. Think teams escaping from Heroku, scaling past Vercel/Fly.io, or trying to escape AWS/GCP/Azure complexity.

We're hiring our first Technical Account Executive. This is a full-cycle role — you'll own outreach, discovery, and demos through to close, partnering closely with the VP Sales.

We're looking for someone with 4–7 years in devtools or cloud infra space who can hold a real conversation with engineers and understand what's actually broken in their setup. A background in cloud/devtools sales or Solutions Engineering but ready to own the whole cycle are both strong fits.

Apply: https://www.linkedin.com/jobs/view/4394700127/ or email steven[at]build.io direct.

We're turning a billion consumers into creators. AI creation as entertainment.

- ~10M creations/month - Daily usage, people come back to build on each other

- Core loop (create → remix → repeat) - Real time generation (latency, cost, scale) - Feed + ranking (what gets seen, what gets built on)

- 50M+ remixes — real behavior, not a demo - Small team, high slope.. engineer #3–4 - Defining a new category: creation as entertainment

author: AREO_Marius
raw_html:
AREO | Remote (EU) | Full-Time | Equity | Roles: [Engineering Manager (Ruby on Rails), Lead Product Engineer (Ruby on Rails)]

My name is Marius and I am the CTO at AREO (https://goareo.com/).

AREO, founded in January 2023 by a team with 20+ years of combined experience in retail and startups, is a young startup specializing in developing innovative solutions for supermarkets and grocery retail stores.

Our cutting-edge B2B SaaS platform optimizes workflows for employees by providing real-time access to inventory, product information, and customer inquiries. We serve large supermarket chains in Germany and across the EU.

About a year ago we brought a backend developer on board through "HN - Who is hiring" and are now seeking a Lead Product Engineer / Engineering Manager with a minimum of 8 years of experience (preferably in startups) as we just closed another large contract with a major retail chain and are growing quickly.

For both the Product Engineer and the Engineering Manager role professional knowledge of German is highly preferred.

If you're passionate about building innovative retail products from scratch, these roles are for you.

Backend Stack: Ruby / Rails (8.x), StimulusJS / Hotwire / Turbo, AlpineJS, Postgres, Docker, DigitalOcean, OpenAPI, LLMs

Perks: Fully remote roles (Europe), work where you are most productive, sizeable equity in a rapidly growing company, flat hierarchies, no-ego work environment, work in a lean but highly experienced team

Apply here: marius[at]goareo.com and mention “HN - Who is hiring?” and the role you are applying for in the subject.

author: platelminto
raw_html:
Formical | Software Engineer | ONSITE (Hybrid - Delft, Netherlands) | Full-time | https://careers.formical.bio

At Formical, we believe we can become the first sustainable generation on Earth. Biotechnology plays a key role in this future - benefiting all people, animals, and our planet by making industrial production more sustainable. But bioprocessing still runs on fragmented data and tacit knowledge. The infrastructure to turn data into the intelligence that teams need to scale doesn't exist yet, hindering the sector’s progress. Our product accelerates bioprocessing by connecting fragmented data, enabling predictive modeling, and ultimately closing the loop between measurement and action.

We're not looking for specific skills, just willingness to learn. I joined a couple months ago with little React/TypeScript full-stack experience, but with CC I managed quite well coming from a Python backend background. Nothing's enforced though! We're a small team, have some customers, and are looking to build out the platform and the client-base significantly this year.

Apply via the link above!

Perry Street Software | Remote (EU/LatAM/NA) | Full time | https://www.perrystreet.com/careers

Perry Street Software publishes Jack'd and SCRUFF, two of the world's largest LGBTQ+ dating apps on iOS and Android, is hiring a Senior Ruby Developer

Our brands reach more than 30 million members worldwide, so members can connect, meet and express themselves on a platform that prioritizes privacy, security, and community. The position is 40 hours per week and ongoing/full-time.

Please mention Hacker news in your cover letter.

author: rmnull
raw_html:
Infystrat | Full Stack Engineer (Java, Microservices) | Remote (India) | Full-Time | 6–10 LPA

We’re hiring early-career engineers (0–2 yrs) to work on open digital infrastructure with real-world impact.

You’ll work on backend systems that power high-throughput services used at population scale, with a focus on privacy, reliability, and interoperability. Most of your work will be open source, contributing to reusable digital public infrastructure adopted by governments and organizations.

What you’ll do: - Build and maintain Java (Spring Boot) microservices - Work on distributed systems and APIs at scale - Contribute to open-source repositories - Collaborate on system design and reliability improvements

Tech stack: Java (Spring Boot), Microservices, Kubernetes Exposure to Digital Public Infrastructure is a plus (can be learned on the job)

What we’re looking for: - Strong fundamentals (CS basics, APIs, data structures) - Ability to learn quickly and work independently - Interest in open source and public-impact systems

To apply: Email: talent_hiring@infystrat.com Subject: HN Hiring – <Your Name> Include: - Resume - GitHub profile (important) - A short note on why this work interests you

author: Estuary
raw_html:
Estuary | US-Based / Remote | Full-time – https://estuary.dev/ Estuary is a VC-backed, post–Series A startup powering right-time data replication and transform — from sub-second CDC replication to terabyte-scale OLAP data flows.

We’re open-source, developer-first, and focused on making data movement radically simpler across both our Platform and Applications layers.

We’re hiring for:

Senior Systems Engineer — Capture & Materializations Design and build high-performance connectors that move data in and out of Estuary in real time. Work across APIs, databases, and streaming systems to ensure correctness, reliability, and scalability. This role is deeply systems-oriented, with a focus on data plane execution. Go and Rust are core to our stack.

Senior Systems Engineer — Control & Data Plane Own core infrastructure that orchestrates and powers Estuary’s runtime — from control plane services to distributed data processing systems. Tackle challenges in scaling, fault tolerance, and performance across a globally distributed system. Strong backend fundamentals required; Go and Rust experience preferred.

Solutions Engineer Work with the founding team to help customers use Estuary effectively. Collaborate closely with engineering and product, shape how users integrate real-time data, and feed insights back into product development.

Product Manager Own product areas end-to-end, working closely with engineering and design to shape the roadmap and deliver a great developer experience. Engage with users, translate real-world use cases into product decisions, and help define how modern data infrastructure should feel to build with.

What we’re looking for: Engineers who care about building reliable, maintainable systems and enjoy working with complex APIs and data flows. We value curiosity, clear communication, and a willingness to learn.

Bonus points for experience with CDC tools, open source contributions, or fast-paced startups.

To apply: Email your resume to careers@estuary.dev

author: PlantingSpace
raw_html:
PlantingSpace | Full-time | Remote (EU time zone) + Quarterly Meet-ups | https://planting.space

We're building a system that represents domain knowledge as modular probabilistic models — making analysis rigorous and transparent. Users can connect these models flexibly into larger structures. The system enforces consistency across them, and propagates uncertainty through each step. Our first applications are in finance and scientific research, with use cases ranging from equity valuation and distress monitoring, to particle physics.

This is an opportunity for builders and practitioners to help shape a deep-tech product from its earliest stage.

Current openings:

* Product Software Engineering (Quantitative Modeling)

* Program Synthesis Engineering

* Applied Category Theory Research

* Bayesian Software Engineering

We're also looking for Domain Experts to collaborate on high-value use cases -- identifying and validating opportunities, shaping them into working solutions with our engineers, and taking them from pilot to production with real users. Practitioners with expertise in Valuation Models, Quantitative Default-Probability Models, or Interest Rate Prediction Models are particularly relevant right now.

Find out more, and apply at: https://planting.space/joinus/

Questions? Get in touch: talent@planting.space
